revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Fujiwara no Taishi (died 794)]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو تایشی (درگذشت ۷۹۴ میلادی)]][[ja:藤原帯子]][[ko:후지와라노 다라시코]][[th:ฟูจิวาระ โนะ ไทชิ (สิ้นพระชนม์ ค.ศ. 794)]][[zh:藤原带子]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0794|6|28}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|1=Emperor Heizei}}|father={{ill|Fujiwara no Momokawa|qid=Q3090640|1=Fujiwara no Momokawa}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Moroane|qid=Q5507531|1=Fujiwara no Moroane}}|family={{ill|Fujiwara clan|qid=Q641628|1=Fujiwara clan}}}}
{{family name hatnote|Fujiwara|lang=Japanese}} '''Fujiwara no Taishi''' (藤原 帯子, also read '''Fujiwara no Tarashiko'''; died 794) was a Japanese [[kuge|noblewoman]] of the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. She was a consort of Prince Ate. She had no children with him, but more than a decade after her death she was granted the title of {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=empress|1=Empress of Japan}} on her husband's becoming {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei|1=Emperor Heizei}}.
== Biography == Fujiwara no Taishi's year of birth is unknown.{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}} Her father was {{ill|Fujiwara no Momokawa|en|Fujiwara no Momokawa|qid=Q3090640|lt=Fujiwara no Momokawa|1=Fujiwara no Momokawa}}.{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}} At some point she married [[:en:Prince Ate|Prince Ate]] (773–824),{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}} but died suddenly of an illness in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku 13|1=Enryaku}} (794).{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}} She was posthumously granted the title of {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=empress|1=Empress of Japan}} (''kōgō'') upon the accession of her husband to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|throne]] in {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō 1|1=Daidō}} (806).{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}} It is thought that the reason she was granted this title over, for example, {{illm|Ise no Tsugiko|ja|伊勢継子}}{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}} (mother of {{illm|Prince Takaoka|ja|高岳親王}}, etc.{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}}) or {{illm|Fujii no Tōshi|ja|葛井藤子}}{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}} (mother of {{ill|Prince Abo|en|Prince Abo|qid=Q11657231|lt=Prince Abo|1=Prince Abo}}{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}}), despite having already died without issue,{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}} was due to the influence of her elder brother, {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|qid=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|qid=Q3090651|lt=Otsugu|1=Fujiwara no Otsugu}}.{{sfnm|1a1=Nishimura|1y=2007}}
== References == === Citations === {{reflist|colwidth=40em}}
=== Works cited === {{Refbegin|colwidth=80em}} * {{cite encyclopedia |last = Nishimura |first = Satomi |author-link = Satomi Nishimura |encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Daijiten |title = Fujiwara no Taishi |script-title = ja:藤原帯子 |language=ja |year = 2007 |publisher = [[Shogakukan]] }} {{Refend}}
{{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}}
[[en:Fujiwara no Taishi (died 794)]]
<!-- https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%97%A4%E5%8E%9F%E5%B8%AF%E5%AD%90-1106118 --><!-- Japanese included since the separate title ''chūgū'' is also "empress". --><!-- This reading comes from Nishimura --><!-- https://researchmap.jp/read0187327/ --> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fa:فوجیوارا نو تایشی (درگذشت ۷۹۴ میلادی)]] [[ja:藤原帯子]] [[ko:후지와라노 다라시코]] [[th:ฟูจิวาระ โนะ ไทชิ (สิ้นพระชนม์ ค.ศ. 794)]] [[zh:藤原带子]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11623232}} [[Category:794 deaths]] [[Category:8th-century empresses consort]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese posthumous empresses]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]]
